sessions.intent: Fix 7 OBP violations + rename identity.getAll to identity.list

before.assumptions: All 7 violations identified in prior analysis are real and fixable without breaking existing tests.
before.risk: Interface changes cascade to all implementations.

sessions.plan: Fix 7 OBP violations + rename identity.getAll → identity.list

actions.context: Non-generic Register to avoid reflection
GetOrCreateDefaultAsync on interface, extracted GenerateIdentity
Sign takes IdentityVariable, extracted ContractsMatch
SerializeData/FormatHash moved to HashedData

sessions.intent: Full 5-pass code analysis of signing/crypto/identity/provider modules

before.assumptions: Coder fixed 7 OBP violations. Analyzing full module set.
before.risk: Large surface area, previous branches had provider return type issues.

sessions.plan: 5-pass analysis: OBP, simplification, readability, behavioral reasoning, deletion test

actions.context: 8 findings: IKeyProvider throws (high), duplication (medium), bare catch (medium)

after.notes: Verdict: fail. Send back to coder.

sessions.intent: Re-review coder's fixes for v1 findings

before.assumptions: Coder addressed top 4 findings. Re-reviewing fix-introduced code for correctness and new issues.
before.risk: Interface change (IKeyProvider) cascades to Ed25519Provider, DefaultIdentityProvider, and 7 test files.

sessions.plan: Verify each fix against original finding. Behavioral reasoning and deletion test on fix-introduced code only.

actions.context: All 4 fixes verified: Data<KeyPair>, generic delegation, ResolveIdentityAsync, narrowed catch. No new issues.

sessions.intent: Test quality analysis of signing/provider/identity/crypto changes

before.assumptions: Codeanalyzer v2 passed. 1795 tests already green. Investigating test quality and coverage gaps.
before.risk: New modules (signing, provider) may have tests that look green but miss critical paths.

sessions.plan: Run tests, collect coverage, analyze test quality: deletion test, weak assertions, missing error-path coverage, PLang test existence

actions.context: Analyzed 97 test methods across 7 test files. Found 6 major coverage gaps and 3 weak assertions.
Wrote test-report.json with 13 findings and coverage data

after.notes: Verdict: needs-fixes. Send back to coder for error-path tests.

sessions.intent: Fix 11 of 13 tester findings: add error-path tests, strengthen assertions, cover untested code

before.assumptions: Tester found 13 issues. 11 are fixable via new tests. 2 skipped (load.cs needs real DLL, PLang tests need builder).
before.risk: Low — only adding tests, no production code changes.

sessions.plan: Add 28 new tests: provider/list action coverage, UnknownType error paths, Ed25519 catch blocks, SignedData.Verify guards, ResolveType all branches, null-name guards, GetOrDefault/Has, SigningSettings defaults. Strengthen 3 weak assertions.

actions.context: 6 new tests: 3 list action, 1 remove UnknownType, 1 setDefault UnknownType
4 new tests: invalid base64 sign/verify, SigningSettings defaults
Strengthened 2 weak assertions with Error.Key checks
4 new SignedData.Verify tests, 1 strengthened assertion
16 new tests: 8 ResolveType, 4 null-name guards, 4 GetOrDefault/Has

after.notes: 28 new tests added. Skipped findings #10 (load.cs) and #12 (PLang tests).

sessions.intent: Re-validate after coder v2 addressed 11/13 tester findings

before.assumptions: Coder added 28 new tests. Need to verify they pass, coverage improved, and test quality is good.

sessions.plan: Run full suite, collect coverage, compare against v1 baseline, review new test quality

actions.context: 28 new tests verified: all pass, coverage gaps closed, assertions check Error.Key
Updated test-report.json: approved. 2 minor remaining.

after.health: PASS — 1827 tests pass, coverage gaps resolved
after.notes: Verdict: approved. Suggest security analyst next.

sessions.intent: Full security audit of signing/crypto/identity/provider modules

before.assumptions: Code analyzer passed v2, tester passed v2 with 1827 tests. Analyzing security posture under PLang's user-sovereign threat model.
before.risk: Crypto modules are security-critical. Incorrect severity ratings could either block good code or pass vulnerable code.

sessions.plan: Blue team: map 6 attack surfaces. Red team: 8 findings with exploit sketches. Align all findings to user-sovereign threat model. Carry-forward check against previous branches.

actions.context: 9-step verification pipeline, ToSigningBytes mutation pattern, nonce replay, contract matching
Crypto implementation, identity persistence, provider registry thread safety
Assembly.LoadFrom RCE assessment — by design under user-sovereign model
Signature property setter, nonce cache, carry-forward Decompress exception gap
8 findings: 0 critical, 0 high, 3 medium, 5 low. Verdict: PASS.

after.notes: Signing pipeline cryptographically sound. Recommend auditor next.

before.assumptions: All three prior reviewers (codeanalyzer, tester, security) gave PASS. Looking for cross-file contract gaps and review quality issues.
before.risk: Large module surface (30+ files, 4 module families) — cross-file contracts are where bugs hide.

sessions.plan: Read all production code, trace cross-file contracts (IdentityData chain, SignedData pipeline, provider registration, ICache.TryAddAsync), assess review quality, run tests.

actions.context: Cross-file contract tracing: SignedData pipeline, IdentityData lazy resolution, Actor.Identity chain
Provider registry contract: registration, resolution, type mapping consistency
Foundation changes: ICache.TryAddAsync, Signature property, SensitivePropertyFilter in envelope options
Audit report: 1 minor, 2 nits. Verdict: PASS.

after.notes: All prior reviewers agreed with. No critical/major cross-file issues. Suggest docs bot next.
